Recipe.name: Char Siu (Chinese BBQ Pork)
Recipe.Notes: Needs overnight marinade — plan ahead. Keep fat on the pork for flavor. Use a metal rack so heat circulates. Watch closely after basting; it burns fast. Internal temp 145°F.

Ingredient.name: boneless pork shoulder/butt, cut into 2–3 inch strips

Steps.Step: Mix sugar, salt, five spice, white pepper, sesame oil, wine, soy sauce, hoisin, molasses, garlic, and food coloring into a marinade. Reserve 2 tbsp separately.
Coat pork strips in remaining marinade. Cover and refrigerate overnight (minimum 8 hours).
Preheat oven to 475°F, rack in upper third. Line a sheet pan with foil, place a metal rack on top. Pour 1½ cups water into the pan below the rack.
Arrange pork on rack with spacing. Roast 25 minutes at 475°F.
Reduce oven to 375°F. Flip pork, add water to pan if dry, rotate pan 180°. Roast another 15 minutes.
Mix reserved marinade with maltose/honey and 1 tbsp hot water for basting sauce.
Baste pork on both sides. Roast final 10 minutes. Optional: broil briefly for extra char — watch carefully.
Baste with remaining sauce. Rest 10 minutes before slicing.
